evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: embed data quality, governance, and observability throughout our data stack
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The quote foregrounds embedding data quality, governance, and observability across the stack, which is quality/governance language rather than shipping-speed language, supporting a rigour classification.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Serving enterprise clients and partners with the loyalty-as-a-service data products implies business-decision stakes tied to external partner relationships, consistent with a high domain-risk rating rather than internal-only tooling.
evidence.collaboration_width: product managers; commercial account managers; engineers (data engineers); data scientists; AI engineers; analysts (business teams)
evidence.data_team_maturity: Design, standardise, and evolve our data modelling (dbt) frameworks
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Designing, standardising, and evolving dbt modelling frameworks describes an established stack that is actively being grown and matured, matching a mid maturity classification rather than a from-scratch or fully mature governance-heavy function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Champion dimensional modelling principles, ensuring our medallion architecture scales efficiently with increasing data volume, variety, and complexity
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The responsibility names a specific methodology (dimensional modelling) and a concrete architectural pattern (medallion architecture) rather than generic boilerplate, indicating a hiring manager wrote the JD.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Empower analysts, engineers, and business teams with high-quality semantic layers and data models that serve as a single source of truth across Ascenda and our partner ecosystem
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The quote explicitly lists analysts, engineers, and business teams as the audience for the semantic layer and single source of truth, which centers internal data consumers rather than one external-facing function.
evidence.autonomy_level: You will shape how our loyalty-as-a-service business models its data, metrics, and data products for enterprise clients and partners
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Shaping how the entire business models its data, metrics, and data products signals setting direction rather than executing pre-scoped tasks, supporting a strategic autonomy classification.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design, standardise, and evolve our data modelling (dbt) frameworks to transform raw data into self-explanatory, performant, and reusable datasets
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Designing, standardising, and evolving existing dbt modelling frameworks describes extending and improving infrastructure that already exists rather than building it from nothing, matching fix_scale.
evidence.ai_role: Work closely with data engineers, scientists, and AI engineers to power machine learning workflows and predictive models
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The role builds data foundations and models that feed machine learning and AI engineering workflows, which is an ai_enabler signal the prior 'none' classification missed.
evidence.testing_framing: embed data quality, governance, and observability throughout our data stack
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Verb 'embed' with 'data quality' establishes direct ownership of implementing quality practices.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: embed data quality, governance, and observability throughout our data stack
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Observability and reliability concerns dominate (moderate framing); governance elements hint at trust but lack explicit regulatory/compliance language.
